task.title: Save validation reports to a directory
task.shortdesc: Use the --output option to write one report file per validated document to a directory. This is useful for sharing results with your team, uploading reports as CI artifacts, or tracking quality over time.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Use the --output option to save one validation report file per Markdown document for sharing with your team or CI artifact upload.

prereq.p: Decide on a format for the saved reports before running the command. Set output format compares the text, JSON, and CSV options.

context.p: Use case by format: JSON — CI artifact upload and scripting; CSV — quality tracking and spreadsheet import; text — ad-hoc team review. The output directory is created automatically if it does not exist.

step.cmd: Choose the report format that matches your use case.

dlentry.dt: JSON — for CI artifact upload and machine processing

dd.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format json --output reports/

dlentry.dt: CSV — for quality tracking and spreadsheet import

dd.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format csv --output reports/

dlentry.dt: Text — for human review and sharing

dd.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format text --output reports/

info.p: Replace reports/ with the path to the directory where you want the report files saved.

step.stepresult: The tool validates every file and writes one report file per document into the output directory. The file extension matches the format: .json, .csv, or .txt.

step.cmd: Open the output directory to confirm the generated files.

info.p: Each report file is named after the source document. For example, validating docs/tutorial-deploy-app.md with --format json produces reports/tutorial-deploy-app.json. With --format csv it produces reports/tutorial-deploy-app.csv.

If you validate a directory, the output directory mirrors only the filenames — subdirectory paths from the source are not recreated. All report files land in the single output directory you specified.

step.cmd: Adopt a naming convention for the output directory if you save reports from multiple runs.

info.p: Including a date or build identifier in the directory name makes it easy to compare reports across runs:
info.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format csv --output reports/2026-03-20/
info.p: For CI pipelines, use the run number or commit SHA to make each set of reports uniquely identifiable.

result.p: One report file per validated document is saved in the output directory in the format you specified. The exit code is 0 if all Required rules pass, or 1 if any Required rule fails — regardless of whether reports are saved.

postreq.p: To upload report files as CI artifacts (so your team can download them from the Actions run summary page), see Add markdown-validator to a GitHub Actions workflow.
